pullRequest.title: make Prometheus optional
pullRequest.body:
I applied the patch proposed in #345 to make the Prometheus middleware optional:

- prometheus-client is now an optional dependency
- The Prometheus middleware is only loaded if the prometheus_client is installed.

fixes #345

pullRequest.title: Bugfix: Change logic of completed_count property for group

pullRequest.title: Improve typing coverage of middleware classes
pullRequest.body:
Closes #521

Following up on comment https://github.com/Bogdanp/dramatiq/pull/731#issuecomment-3069279831. Alas, I am not familiar enough with Sphinx to work with the `NameError` caused by the missing (typing only) imports (perhaps related issue https://github.com/sphinx-doc/sphinx/issues/13137).

pullRequest.title: Fix/rabbitmq pika logging
pullRequest.body: Closes https://github.com/Bogdanp/dramatiq/issues/559

pullRequest.title: Add documentation of asyncio support
pullRequest.body: Closes #595

pullRequest.title: Change `compute_backoff` implementation to respect min_backoff
pullRequest.body:
Closes #651

Currently, because the 'jittering' makes the `backoff` smaller, for the first attempt, the returned value will be smaller than `factor` - which is `min_backoff`. This is the bug reported.

My change here is to make the 'jittering' _increase_ the backoff, so it is never smaller than `factor` (`min_backoff`).

This implementation does mean there will be no jittering once max_backoff is reached.
Is that a problem? @Bogdanp Can I get your opinion?

pullRequest.title: Add example of using actors from different files
pullRequest.body:
Example of using actors from different files.

Issue: https://github.com/Bogdanp/dramatiq/issues/676

pullRequest.title: Improve Documentation about message priorities
pullRequest.body:
Closes #724 

* Make limitations of Actor `priority` option clear. 
* Improve examples of RabbitMQ `broker_priority`.
* Link to the RabbitMQ `broker_priority` example from the guide.

pullRequest.title: Improve typing coverage on external API, in particular Worker and Broker classes
pullRequest.body:
Closes #727

Adds a bunch of type annotations to many public functions; in two cases the annotations would require a minor rewrite of code itself.

I’ve not annotated `message` arguments because they can be either of type `Message` or `MessageProxy` (because they’re used interchangeably). I also refrained from importing modules guarded by `if TYPE_CHECKING` but I can add that too and further improve some method signatures. Please let me know what you prefer!

pullRequest.title: Change StubBroker.join() parameter fail_fast to default to True
pullRequest.body:
When `fail_fast=True` (now the default) and any message gets dead-lettered during the join, then an exception will be raised.

Resolves #739

pullRequest.body: Closes https://github.com/Bogdanp/dramatiq/issues/748

pullRequest.title: redis: Remove worker heartbeat compatibility code
pullRequest.body:
This partially reverts commit d6cff30701c7338ce1e4ef3546ba2fab9fe12bd9.

The heartbeat logic had been changed in [v1.2.0](https://github.com/Bogdanp/dramatiq/releases/tag/v1.2.0), so removing this is a breaking change only for those who would be upgrading from [v1.1.0](https://github.com/Bogdanp/dramatiq/releases/tag/v1.1.0) - which released more than 7 years ago - directly to v2.

Closes #770

pullRequest.title: chore: fix unnecesary rate limit backend warnings
pullRequest.body: Closes https://github.com/Bogdanp/dramatiq/issues/787

pullRequest.title: Fix #791: Fix infinite loop when async actor raises `TimeoutError` exception
pullRequest.body:
Check that future is not done before continuing with while loop. 
This fixes an infinite loop when a TimeoutError is raised inside the future being waited on.

Fixes https://github.com/Bogdanp/dramatiq/issues/791

pullRequest.title: Fix #795: Fix typing of `actor` decorator to work with async actors
chore: update documentation to include all extras
pullRequest.body: Closes https://github.com/Bogdanp/dramatiq/issues/797

pullRequest.title: Recognize the --watch arg even when watchdog not installed
pullRequest.body:
This PR makes it so a useful error message is printed when --watch is used without watchdog installed, rather than a generic "unknown argument" error.

Before:

```text
$ dramatiq --watch . app
usage: dramatiq [-h] [--processes PROCESSES] [--threads THREADS] [--path [PATH ...]] [--queues [QUEUES ...]] [--pid-file PID_FILE] [--log-file LOG_FILE] [--skip-logging] [--use-spawn] [--fork-function FORKS] [--worker-fork-timeout WORKER_FORK_TIMEOUT] [--worker-shutdown-timeout WORKER_SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT]
                [--version] [--verbose]
                broker [module ...]
dramatiq: error: unrecognized arguments: --watch
```

After:

```text
$ dramatiq --watch . app
[2026-02-08 16:38:01,952] [PID 8793] [MainThread] [dramatiq.MainProcess] [CRITICAL] Watching for source changes requires dramatiq be to installed with the extra 'watch' e.g. pip install 'dramatiq[watch]'
```

Closes #798

pullRequest.title: Rename `ConnectionError` class to `BrokerConnectionError`
pullRequest.body:
This is to avoid overwriting the built-in exception class `ConnectionError`

Closes #800

pullRequest.title: Add *args and **kwargs to base generic actor class perform() method
pullRequest.body:
This ensures the correct `NotImplementedError` is raised when called with args.

Shouldn't effect real code using this, since they should implement their own `perform()` method,
meaning this code is never actually called.

Closes #805

pullRequest.title: Improvements to dynamic rate limit/result backend class lookup
pullRequest.body:
* Raise Attribute error from `__getattr__` as python docs recommend: https://docs.python.org/3/reference/datamodel.html#module.__getattr__
* Increase warning `stacklevel` by 1 so warning comes from the code doing the importing.

Closes #821

pullRequest.title: Add compatibility with redis-py 7.X
pullRequest.body: Closes #824

pullRequest.title: Detect deleted rabbitMQ queues and re-declare them
pullRequest.body:
Closes #832 

This PR ensures that queues are re-declared if they are deleted while rabbitMQ is running.

It detects missing queues in two places:
1. When consuming from them. In this case an error with `reply_code==404` will be raised. This is now checked in the consumer.
2. When publishing a message. NOTE this case is only possible when `confirm_delivery` is turned on. When this is the case, the `mandatory` flag is set, which causes an `UnroutableError` to be raised. This is checked for.

In both cases the missing queue is re-added to `queues_pending`. This causes it to be re-declared the next time `_ensure_queue` is called.

pullRequest.title: Fix: ensure that StubBroker.flush_all does not break existing consumers
pullRequest.body: Fixes #840
